message:
refactor(scheduler): remove redundant single-element peek-then-clear fast paths in pop()

The three-lane scheduler's pop() method had special-case branches that
checked `lane.len() == 1`, then used peek() + clear() instead of falling
through to the normal pop() path. These fast paths were logically
redundant: BinaryHeap::pop() on a single-element heap is already O(1)
with no sift-down needed, so the peek-then-clear shortcut provided no
measurable benefit while doubling the code surface and branching.

Removing the three redundant blocks simplifies the method from 18 lines
to 6 lines of logic with identical behavior and performance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
